skill.name: reflect-on-work
skill.description: Guides an AI agent to add concise work reflections and knowledge updates after completing a task.
skill.summary: Adds structured reflection notes after completed AI agent work.
skill.author: Clouder0

security_audit.summary: Static command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance findings are false positives caused by Markdown JSON examples and ordinary prose. No executable code, network access, filesystem writes, secret access, or real shell commands were found. The remaining concern is semantic: the skill instructs agents to add mandatory reflection output to every response, which can override normal response formatting.

medium_findings.title: Broad Agent Output Override
medium_findings.description: The skill says it is required for all agent outputs and that every agent output must include specific reflection fields. This is not malware, but it can override normal task-specific response formats and should be disclosed before publication.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The mandatory wording is explicit and repeated in the skill content. The risk is behavioral rather than executable, so confidence is high for the issue but not critical.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Markdown JSON Fences
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the locations are Markdown code fences around JSON examples. I found no evidence of shell execution or command interpolation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines are fenced JSON examples in Markdown. They are not executable Ruby, shell scripts, or tool instructions.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography at prose locations. The referenced lines describe the skill and a knowledge category, and they contain no cryptographic algorithm or hashing operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural language only. I found no MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged system reconnaissance at a line that introduces a minimal output example. I found no evidence of host inspection, process listing, environment probing, or system information collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is ordinary documentation text. It does not request or perform operating system reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose useful context after an AI agent finishes a task. This skill captures concise lessons, failures, and patterns so future work can improve.
actual_capabilities: Defines reflection fields for what worked, what failed, and patterns noticed.
Provides categories for codebase, convention, decision, and gotcha knowledge updates.
Defines confidence levels for captured knowledge.
Shows compact examples for post-task reflection output.
Encourages concise and specific lessons after task completion.
limitations: Does not execute commands, inspect files, or validate project state.
Can conflict with tasks that require a specific output format.
Does not store reflections outside the current agent response.
Requires users or agents to judge which lessons are accurate.

use_cases.title: Capture Engineering Lessons
use_cases.description: Record codebase patterns, useful tactics, and pitfalls after a development task.

use_cases.title: Improve Agent Handoffs
use_cases.description: Add short reflections that help the next agent continue work with better context.

use_cases.title: Review Repeated Workflows
use_cases.description: Collect recurring patterns that may become future skills, checks, or team guidelines.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Task Reflection
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after a simple completed task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Reflect on the completed task. Include what worked, what failed, and any patterns noticed.

prompt_templates.title: Knowledge Capture
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after learning new project details.
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize the task result and capture any codebase, convention, decision, or gotcha knowledge with confidence levels.

prompt_templates.title: Handoff Reflection
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when another agent or teammate may continue the work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a concise handoff reflection that explains useful context, failed approaches, and repeatable patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Workflow Improvement Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after a complex multi-step task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the full workflow. Identify successful strategies, wasted effort, hidden assumptions, and patterns that could improve future work.

output_examples.input: A developer finishes adding a login validation fix.
output_examples.output: The response includes a short reflection noting that existing tests guided the change, an early file search was misleading, and validation helpers are reused across auth code.

output_examples.input: An agent completes a repository exploration task.
output_examples.output: The response captures discovered architecture details, marks confidence for each lesson, and lists patterns that help the next task start faster.

output_examples.input: A team lead reviews repeated agent work.
output: Useful patterns are summarized for reuse.
Failed approaches are named without blame.
Potential future skill ideas are identified.

best_practices: Use the skill after completing a meaningful task, not during every small message.
Keep reflections brief, specific, and tied to observed evidence.
Mark uncertain lessons clearly so future users know what needs verification.
anti_patterns: Do not add reflection fields when the user requires a strict response format.
Do not present guesses as certain knowledge updates.
Do not let reflection content replace the actual task result.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps an AI agent capture useful lessons after completing work.

faq.question: Does it run commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill is documentation only and allows reading context.

faq.question: Can it change files?
faq.answer: No evidence found that it edits files or writes stored notes.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: It uses broad mandatory output instructions that can conflict with normal response formatting.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it after a completed task when lessons or project patterns are worth preserving.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
